summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a 30KB crafted protobuf binary with 15,000 nested wire type 3 (start group) fields causes an uncontrolled recursion in Reader.skipType, resulting in RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded and crashing the decoder.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the crafted protobuf payload to trigger the recursive skipType code path and produce a RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded, crashing the Node.js process inside the container.
actual_observed_result: Running the exploit trigger against the protobuf.js target repository causes Type.decode() to throw RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded when processing the deeply nested wire type 3 groups. The proof string EVOHUNT_PROOF: stack_overflow_in_skipType_via_wire_type_3 is emitted.
verification_method: The exploit script constructs a 30,001-byte protobuf binary with 15,000 nested start-group tags (0x13) and 15,000 end-group tags (0x14), decodes it using Type.decode(), and checks whether the caught error is a RangeError (stack overflow). The script distinguishes the stack overflow from normal parsing errors and exits with code 0 only when the proof signal is confirmed.
evidence_produced_by_run: The exploit script throws RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded when decoding the crafted protobuf binary.
The error is caught and the proof string EVOHUNT_PROOF: stack_overflow_in_skipType_via_wire_type_3 is emitted to stdout.
The verification confirms the error originates from the recursive skipType(wireType) code path in src/reader.js:371-374.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
